title: cracca coronilloides
intro: Cracca coronilloides, also known as False Indigo, is a perennial plant native to North America, characterized by its blue-violet flowers and compound leaves. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are derived from its alkaloids and glycosides. The health benefits of Cracca coronilloides include treating skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues, as well as reducing fever and pain. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
intro_study: According to "Antioxidants (Basel, Switzerland)", Crataegus coronilloides, similar to its related species, has been found to possess cardiotonic properties and exhibit vascular reactivity, as well as anti-inflammatory and anti-nociceptive activities, due to the presence of compounds like quercetin 3-O-rhamnoside, quercetin 3-O-β-galactoside, and kaempferol 3-O-α-L-rhamnoside.
